page: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-8 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the E2 family, configured with 8 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM. The underlying hardware features an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor with 4 physical cores, supporting 2 threads per core with dedicated CPU allocation. The processor includes 16 MB of L3 cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. It supports instruction sets such as AVX, AES, and SHA_NI, but lacks AVX-512 and AMX extensions. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong single-core CPU capability, with stress-ng single-core performance ranking in the top 25%. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics generally fall within the average tier. The instance achieved average ratings in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, as well as in Passmark memory and database operations. A notable performance bottleneck is observed in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, which ranks in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the e2-highmem-8 provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-performance local storage or GPU acceleration. The combination of dedicated AMD EPYC cores and a substantial memory footprint supports workloads such as database operations, Redis caching, static web serving, and software compilation.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor with 4 physical cores and dedicated CPU allocation. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests, while multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. Conversely, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is a relative bottleneck, placing in the bottom 25%. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core, this instance offers a cost-effective profile for memory-heavy applications. It is suitable for workloads such as Redis caching, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-8 is an x86_64 server with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B12 CPU. It offers strong single-core performance but average multi-core and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-8 is an x86_64 server featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and dedicated AMD EPYC 7B12 cores without local storage.
tagline: An x86_64 instance offering 64 GB of RAM and dedicated AMD EPYC cores for memory-intensive workloads requiring strong single-core performance.
